straight.disc_explanation: Stable driver on a flat release tracks straight with a gentle fade at the end. No wind means no compensation needed.
tips: Use your natural release angle.
Focus on a clean follow-through.
A stable mid is a safer option if accuracy matters more than distance.

short_left.disc_explanation: Overstable mid naturally fades left at the end, producing a short left finish without needing a hyzer angle.
short_left.aim_point: Right of target by 5-10 feet depending on distance
tips: Let the disc's natural fade do the work.
Don't over-muscle it — overstable discs punish power with extra fade.
A putter on hyzer is an alternative for tight short-left shots.

short_right.disc_explanation: Understable mid on anhyzer turns right and holds there on a short shot, finishing right of the target.
tips: Keep power moderate — too much speed and it won't turn enough.
A stable disc on a slight anhyzer also works here.
Watch for the disc to snap back left if thrown too hard.

long_left.disc_explanation: Overstable driver thrown flat carries distance then fades hard left at the end, producing a long left finish.
long_left.aim_point: 10-20 feet right of target depending on distance
tips: More power = more distance before the fade kicks in.
Slight hyzer release accelerates the fade earlier if needed.
Avoid anhyzer — overstable discs will fight it and still fade left.

long_right.disc_explanation: Understable driver on anhyzer turns right and carries long before fading back left slightly. For a true long-right finish, keep anhyzer aggressive.
tips: High arm speed maximizes the turn.
If disc comes back too much, increase anhyzer angle.
Best thrown on a slight downslope for maximum right carry.

s_curve.disc_explanation: Understable driver thrown flat will turn right (the S's first curve) then fade back left (the return). Perfect S-curve flight.
s_curve.aim_point: Slightly left of target — let the disc work back to center
tips: This is the money shot for understable discs.
A stable disc at high speed can also produce a mild S-curve.
Great for getting around obstacles on both sides.

distance.disc_explanation: Understable driver maximizes glide and distance potential by turning slightly right and floating longer before fading.
distance.aim_point: Slightly left of landing zone to account for fade
tips: Higher release angle (not too much anhyzer) adds height and hang time.
Match disc stability to your arm speed — overpowering understable discs loses control.
Hyzer-flip technique: throw understable on hyzer so it flips flat and glides far.

putting.disc_explanation: Stable putter on a flat release gives the most predictable flight to the basket. No wind means no compensation.
tips: Keep your release consistent and repeatable.
Follow through toward the basket.
Overstable putters are better for longer putts where wind can creep in.

straight.disc_explanation: Uphill shots naturally produce more lift and the disc acts more understable. A stable mid compensates and stays on line.
tips: The uphill grade makes the disc feel like it has more turn — account for this.
Use slightly more overstable than you think you need.
Power up slightly to maintain disc speed through the climb.

short_left.disc_explanation: Uphill fights overstability slightly, so a more overstable disc on flat-to-hyzer will still reliably fade left.
tips: Don't be afraid of the overstable — the uphill neutralizes some of the fade.
Hyzer angle locks in the left finish regardless of grade.

short_right.disc_explanation: Uphill + anhyzer on a stable disc turns right reliably since uphill encourages turn already.
tips: Be careful not to overdo the anhyzer — uphill amplifies the turn.
Understable disc + uphill + anhyzer can get away from you fast.

long_left.disc_explanation: Uphill neutralizes some overstability. A flat overstable driver carries up and fades hard left at end.
tips: Power through the shot — distance is reduced uphill.
The fade will still happen, just slightly later than on flat ground.

long_right.disc_explanation: Uphill + understable + anhyzer gives a strong right-turning flight that holds the anhyzer angle longer.
tips: Be very mindful of over-turning — this combo is aggressive.
A stable disc on anhyzer uphill might be the safer play.

s_curve.disc_explanation: Stable driver uphill turns slightly right (uphill amplifies), then fades back left for an S-curve. No need for understable here.
tips: The grade does some of the work — choose slightly more stable than flat ground.
Understable uphill can over-turn into a full roller or extreme right finish.

distance.disc_explanation: Uphill costs distance. A stable driver maximizes distance by flying straight with minimal unnecessary fade.
tips: Add 10-20% more power to compensate for uphill distance loss.
Don't overshoot with an understable disc — the uphill amplifies the turn.

putting.disc_explanation: Uphill putts require more power. Stable putter stays on line with added power without fading unpredictably.
putting.aim_point: High center of chains — the disc will drop as it climbs
tips: Aim higher on the basket than normal.
Follow through aggressively — it's easy to come up short uphill.
Overstable putter for windy uphill putts; stable for calm.

straight.disc_explanation: Downhill makes discs act more overstable. Use an overstable mid to fight this tendency and fly straighter.
tips: Power down slightly — discs carry farther downhill.
Overstable keeps the disc on line when the downhill grade wants to push it left.

short_left.disc_explanation: Downhill already promotes fade. An overstable putter on hyzer locks in a reliable left finish at short range.
tips: This combo fades reliably — don't overthrow.
The downhill slope acts like a hyzer booster.

short_right.disc_explanation: Downhill promotes overstability, so understable + anhyzer counters that tendency and produces a right finish.
tips: The understable disc fights the downhill overstability — they cancel out somewhat.
Watch for the disc to snap back left if the disc runs out of speed downhill.

long_left.disc_explanation: Downhill + hyzer on a stable driver produces a strong left-finishing shot with good distance.
tips: Don't use overstable — downhill already adds effective overstability.
Hyzer angle + grade = reliable hard fade.

long_right.disc_explanation: Downhill naturally promotes overstability, so understable on flat counters this and produces a right-biased flight.
tips: Flat or slight anhyzer — downhill fights overstability.
Watch for the disc to come back hard left at the end if understability runs out.

s_curve.disc_explanation: Downhill + overstable + anhyzer: the disc turns right (anhyzer/speed), then overstability fights back for a left fade — classic S-curve.
s_curve.aim_point: Slightly left of where you want to land
tips: This is an advanced shot — the disc needs enough speed to hold the anhyzer before fading.
Power down slightly — downhill adds speed.

distance.disc_explanation: Downhill adds significant distance. Overstable driver prevents runaway turns and keeps the disc on a controlled long flight.
tips: Power down 10-20% — downhill does the work.
Overstable prevents the disc from turning over on the extended flight.

putting.disc_explanation: Downhill putts fly farther and can fade more than expected. Overstable putter stays on line at lower speeds.
putting.aim_point: Lower center of chains — the disc carries farther than expected
tips: Power down — it's easy to overshoot downhill putts.
Aim at the base of the chains, not the center.
Overstable resists unexpected fades at the end of the putt.

straight.disc_explanation: Headwind amplifies lift and makes the disc act more understable. Overstable driver fights the wind's tendency to flip the disc.
tips: Power down — headwinds add lift and the disc floats unpredictably at full power.
Lower release angles cut through the wind better — avoid high loft.
Grip firm to prevent wobble at release.

short_left.disc_explanation: Headwind amplifies fade. Overstable mid on hyzer reliably dumps left early in the flight.
tips: This is a safe, controlled shot — headwind + hyzer + overstable is very predictable.
Don't power up into the wind — it will fight you.

short_right.disc_explanation: Headwind wants to flip the disc, so a stable disc on slight anhyzer uses the wind's flipping force to go right before fading back.
tips: Don't use understable in a headwind anhyzer — it will turn over wildly.
This shot requires precision — the headwind makes anhyzer risky.

long_left.disc_explanation: Headwind + overstable + hyzer = strong left finish with controlled distance. The most reliable long-left shot in a headwind.
tips: Power down 20-30% in a headwind — the disc will still travel.
Low release to cut under the wind.

long_right.disc_explanation: Headwind amplifies turning. A stable driver on anhyzer turns right aggressively and carries. Don't use understable — it will flip.
tips: This is a risky shot in a strong headwind — consider whether it's necessary.
Watch power — headwind amplifies turn unpredictably.

s_curve.disc_explanation: Overstable on anhyzer in a headwind: disc turns right due to anhyzer/headwind lift, then overstability fights back for a left fade.
tips: Advanced shot — headwind makes this difficult to control.
A stable disc might produce a more reliable S-curve here.

distance.disc_explanation: Distance is compromised in a headwind. Overstable driver keeps the disc from flipping while maximizing controlled distance.
distance.aim_point: Left of landing zone to account for fade
tips: Low line — punch the disc through the wind, don't loft it.
Accept 20-40% distance loss. Control beats distance in a headwind.
Hyzer-flip with an overstable disc is an option for extra distance.

putting.disc_explanation: Headwind slows the putt and can push it off-line. Overstable putter maintains its line and fights the wind.
putting.aim_point: High on chains — wind will push the disc down
tips: Step closer if legal — headwind putts are one of the hardest shots.
Drive through the putt. Commit fully.
Aim higher than normal to compensate for wind drag.

straight.disc_explanation: Headwind + uphill = double understable effect on the disc. Overstable driver fights both forces to fly straight.
tips: This is the hardest condition to throw straight in.
Power up slightly for the uphill, but controlled — headwind fights you.
Punch through with a low, flat release.

short_left.disc_explanation: Both headwind and uphill encourage understability. Overstable + hyzer locks in the left fade despite both factors.
tips: Trust the disc — hyzer + overstable will fade left reliably.
Power through for uphill distance.

short_right.disc_explanation: Headwind + uphill both push disc toward understable behavior. A flat overstable mid resists the flip and lands in a mild right position.
tips: True short-right shots are very difficult uphill into a headwind.
Work with the conditions and try anhyzer very carefully.

long_left.disc_explanation: Overstable driver on hyzer into a headwind uphill — the most controlled long-left option in tough conditions.
tips: This is a grind shot — expect reduced distance.
Consistent hyzer angle is key. Stay low and punch through.

long_right.disc_explanation: Headwind + uphill will flip the disc right aggressively. A stable driver on anhyzer rides this flip for a long-right flight.
tips: Very risky — easy to turn over completely.
Only attempt if you need this line — otherwise layup.

s_curve.disc_explanation: Uphill + headwind flip the disc right first, then overstability brings it back left for an S-curve.
tips: The headwind and uphill do the turning — use them.
Power through to maintain disc speed uphill.

distance.disc_explanation: Distance is severely limited. Overstable driver maximizes what distance is achievable without flipping.
tips: Accept reduced distance. Control and placement matter more.
Low release to cut through the headwind.
Extra power for uphill, but don't sacrifice disc angle.

putting.disc_explanation: Uphill + headwind means you must drive through the putt with power. Overstable putter won't deflect off-line.
tips: Commit fully and punch it — the wind and uphill both steal momentum.
Aim higher than you think. The headwind kills elevation fast.
Overstable putter prevents last-second deflection.

straight.disc_explanation: Headwind wants to flip, downhill adds stability — opposing forces. Overstable driver threads the middle.
tips: Power down — downhill + headwind combo adds distance anyway.
Flat release gives the best controlled flight.

short_left.disc_explanation: Downhill adds stability, headwind adds lift — overstable mid on flat finds the left finish balance.
tips: Don't over-hyzer — downhill and overstable are already fading left.
Power down — disc travels far downhill.

short_right.disc_explanation: Headwind flips the disc toward right, downhill adds stability. Stable disc on anhyzer finds the middle ground for a right finish.
tips: The headwind's flipping force works in your favor here.
Downhill prevents the flip from getting out of control.

long_left.disc_explanation: Downhill + headwind + overstable + hyzer = very strong left finish with extended distance from the downhill.
tips: Power way down — this combo produces a very long flight.
Watch for the disc to fade sharply early in a strong headwind.

long_right.disc_explanation: Headwind flips the disc right, downhill extends flight. Understable on flat rides both forces for a long right flight.
tips: Risky — headwind + understable can turn over violently.
Use stable disc instead if conditions are severe.

s_curve.disc_explanation: Headwind flips disc toward right early in flight (first S curve), then as speed drops downhill, the stable disc fades left.
tips: Let the headwind be your turning force.
The disc must have enough speed for the turn — don't power down too much.

distance.disc_explanation: Downhill adds distance, headwind steals it back — these roughly cancel. Overstable maximizes controlled distance while preventing turn-over.
tips: These two forces roughly cancel — treat it like a flat headwind shot.
Low release, firm grip.

putting.disc_explanation: Downhill extends the putt distance, headwind fights it. Overstable putter stays on line.
tips: Power through the headwind.
Downhill makes it easy to be short — commit to the putt.
Overstable prevents unexpected deflection.

straight.disc_explanation: Tailwind reduces effective stability — the disc acts more understable. Overstable driver compensates and flies straight.
tips: Tailwind helps distance but can cause understable discs to flip wildly.
Use one step up in overstability from what you'd throw in calm conditions.
Release at slightly less power — the wind adds speed for you.

short_left.disc_explanation: Tailwind reduces overstability. A stable mid on hyzer gives the reliable left fade that an overstable disc would in calm conditions.
tips: Don't reach for overstable — the tailwind already helps the fade.
Hyzer angle + tailwind speed = reliable left finish.

short_right.disc_explanation: Tailwind amplifies turn — understable on anhyzer produces an exaggerated right finish. Great shot when you need to go right fast.
tips: Power down — tailwind already adds speed.
Watch for the disc to over-turn past your target.

long_left.disc_explanation: Tailwind reduces stability. Stable driver on flat-to-hyzer flies far with a left fade. The tailwind adds distance.
tips: The tailwind does the work — don't overthrow.
A flat release lets the tailwind push the disc for maximum carry.

long_right.disc_explanation: Tailwind + understable + anhyzer is a distance monster to the right. The disc turns hard right and carries extremely far.
tips: Be very careful of over-turning — this combo can produce a turnover.
Use a stable disc if the wind is strong and you need the shot to come back.

s_curve.disc_explanation: Tailwind makes disc act understable. An overstable driver on flat turns slightly right first (from tailwind effect), then fades back left.
tips: The tailwind creates the S's first curve automatically.
Let the disc's fade bring it back — don't force the turn.

distance.disc_explanation: Tailwind is your best distance friend. Understable driver on flat + tailwind = maximum distance. Disc glides far before fading.
distance.aim_point: Slightly left of landing zone for fade
tips: Best conditions for distance records.
Power down slightly — let the wind do the work.
Watch for turnover — the tailwind can push understable discs past the point of recovery.

putting.disc_explanation: Tailwind pushes the disc through — overstable putter won't be pushed past the basket and stays on line.
putting.aim_point: Lower center of chains — tailwind carries it further than you think
tips: Aim lower — the disc will carry farther than normal.
Power down on your putt — tailwind adds momentum.
Overstable prevents the tailwind from pushing the disc past the chains.

straight.disc_explanation: Tailwind reduces stability and uphill also promotes understability. Both combine — stable driver on flat is the right call.
tips: Overstable might be needed in a strong tailwind uphill.
Power up for the uphill — the tailwind helps a little but not enough for climbing.

short_left.disc_explanation: Uphill + tailwind both reduce effective stability. Overstable mid on flat provides the left fade.
tips: Uphill eats into the tailwind boost — power up.
Overstable fights both the uphill and tailwind understable effects.

short_right.disc_explanation: Uphill + tailwind both promote understability (right turn). Even a flat release with understable disc curves right reliably.
tips: You may not even need anhyzer — let the conditions do the work.
Watch for an aggressive turnover.

long_left.disc_explanation: Uphill + tailwind both fight stability. Overstable driver on flat overcomes both effects for a left-finishing long shot.
tips: Power through — uphill costs distance and the tailwind doesn't fully compensate.
Trust the overstable fade.

long_right.disc_explanation: Uphill + tailwind are both understable forces. Understable driver on flat aggressively turns right and carries far.
tips: This is an aggressive combination — control is difficult.
Use stable if you need reliability.

s_curve.disc_explanation: Overstable driver in tailwind uphill: the combined understable forces turn it right first, then overstability fades it left.
tips: The conditions create the S without much help from you.
Stay flat — let the disc do the work.

distance.disc_explanation: Uphill kills distance; tailwind gives some back. Stable driver maximizes efficient uphill distance without flipping.
tips: Accept reduced distance — uphill + tailwind still means less than flat calm.
Stable disc prevents the double-understable conditions from flipping the disc.

putting.disc_explanation: Uphill requires power, tailwind helps — stable putter balances both for a controlled putt.
tips: The tailwind helps push the disc uphill — aim high and commit.
Stable prevents unpredictable late fade.

straight.disc_explanation: Downhill adds stability, tailwind reduces it — they partially cancel. Overstable driver leans into the downhill's natural stability to fly straight.
tips: Power way down — tailwind + downhill adds massive distance.
Overstable prevents the tailwind from flipping the disc on a long downhill carry.

short_left.disc_explanation: Downhill adds extra distance and stability. Stable driver on hyzer produces a reliable left fade before the disc carries too far.
tips: Power way down — this can go very far.
Hyzer angle ensures the left finish before the disc runs out.

short_right.disc_explanation: Tailwind promotes right turn; downhill extends the flight. Understable on flat glides right for a while.
tips: Tremendous distance risk — this combo carries very far.
Consider a mid disc for more control on short shots.

long_left.disc_explanation: Tailwind + downhill = huge distance. Stable driver fades left naturally. Don't need overstable — the distance does it.
tips: Power way down — distance is almost automatic here.
Let the disc's natural fade bring it left at the end.

long_right.disc_explanation: Tailwind + downhill + understable = maximum right carry. This is an extreme distance shot to the right.
tips: Extreme distance potential — placement is the challenge.
Stable disc if you need more control.

s_curve.disc_explanation: Tailwind makes stable disc act understable (right turn first). Downhill extends the flight, then stable fade brings it left — long S-curve.
tips: Great distance S-curve conditions.
Power down — tailwind + downhill = maximum carry.

distance.disc_explanation: Maximum distance conditions. Overstable prevents flip-over on the extended carry. Tailwind + downhill = furthest possible controlled flight.
distance.aim_point: Left of landing zone to account for fade
tips: Best possible distance conditions.
Power down significantly — let the conditions work for you.
Overstable is critical to prevent a turnover in this combo.

putting.disc_explanation: Tailwind + downhill = way too much power gets to the disc. Overstable putter resists being pushed past the basket.
putting.aim_point: Low chains — disc will carry much farther than expected
tips: Power way down — this putt will fly.
Aim at the bottom of the basket.
Overstable prevents the tailwind from carrying the disc past the chains.

straight.disc_explanation: Left-to-right wind pushes the disc right. Overstable driver's natural left fade counters the rightward push to maintain a straight flight.
straight.aim_point: Slightly left of target — let the wind and fade cancel each other
tips: Aim into the wind — left of target — and let it drift to center.
Overstable fade cancels the wind push for a net-straight flight.
Strong L-to-R wind: aim further left and use more overstable.

short_left.disc_explanation: Left-to-right wind works against a left finish. Overstable on hyzer forces the left finish despite the rightward push.
short_left.aim_point: Well right of target — wind pushes left finish back left
tips: Aim much further right than you think.
The wind pushes right, the overstable hyzer pulls left — aim for the middle.
Strong wind may require aiming almost entirely into the wind.

short_right.disc_explanation: Left-to-right wind naturally pushes the disc right — use a stable mid and let the wind do the right-curve work for you.
short_right.aim_point: Left of target — wind drifts disc right
tips: The wind is your friend for right shots — don't fight it.
No anhyzer needed — the wind handles the right drift.
Understable disc would over-turn.

long_left.disc_explanation: Left-to-right wind fights the left finish. Overstable + hyzer ensures the disc beats the wind and finishes left.
long_left.aim_point: Far right of target — you're fighting the wind all the way
tips: This is a difficult shot — you're fighting the wind the whole flight.
Aim well into the wind and expect a longer carry due to wind resistance.

long_right.disc_explanation: Left-to-right wind + understable disc = aggressive right push. Wind adds to the understable turn for a long right flight.
long_right.aim_point: Left of target — wind and understability push it right
tips: The wind works with you on this shot — aim left and let it carry right.
Easy to over-turn — stable disc might be safer.

s_curve.disc_explanation: Left-to-right pushes disc right first (first S curve), then overstable fade brings it left. Natural S-curve from wind conditions.
tips: The L-to-R wind creates the S's right curve without a special throw.
Overstable ensures the fade brings it back left.

distance.disc_explanation: Left-to-right wind has less effect on distance than head/tailwind. Stable driver with slight overstability prevents the disc from pushing too far right.
tips: Adjust aim left by the amount of wind drift you expect.
Use a stable to slightly overstable disc to maintain the line.

putting.disc_explanation: Left-to-right wind pushes the putt right. Overstable putter fights the drift and stays on a left-biased line.
putting.aim_point: Left of center chains — let the wind drift it to center
tips: Aim left of the basket center.
Overstable putter fights the rightward push.
Shorter stance in crosswind for better balance.

straight.disc_explanation: Uphill adds understable effect; L-to-R pushes right. Double right-push conditions — overstable counters both.
tips: Aim left — both uphill and wind push right.
Overstable driver is essential here.

short_left.disc_explanation: Uphill + L-to-R = double right push. Overstable hyzer fights both for a left finish.
tips: This is a tough shot — overstable hyzer is essential.
Power up for uphill.

short_right.disc_explanation: Uphill + L-to-R wind both encourage right. Stable disc on flat rides both forces right without needing anhyzer.
tips: Let the conditions do the work.
Very little disc manipulation needed — the forces push right naturally.

long_left.disc_explanation: Three forces to fight: uphill, L-to-R, long distance. Overstable hyzer is the only answer.
tips: This may not be achievable in strong wind — consider a safer line.
Power through both the uphill and wind.

long_right.disc_explanation: Uphill + L-to-R + understable = three right-pushing forces. This disc will go far right — control carefully.
tips: Extreme right push — very difficult to control.
Stable disc might be more reliable here.

s_curve.disc_explanation: Wind and uphill push right first (S start), then overstable fight brings it left at end.
tips: The conditions create the S naturally.
Power through for uphill.

distance.disc_explanation: Uphill + L-to-R both push right. Overstable keeps disc on a forward line, preventing a hard right drift.
tips: Accept reduced distance uphill.
Aim left and power through.

putting.disc_explanation: Uphill + L-to-R = power-up putt with wind drift. Overstable putter compensates for both.
tips: Aim high and left — uphill needs height, wind drifts right.
Commit and power through.

straight.disc_explanation: Downhill adds stability, L-to-R pushes right — they partially offset. Overstable driver stays straight.
tips: Power way down — downhill + wind = lots of distance.
Aim slightly left to compensate for wind drift.

short_left.disc_explanation: Downhill adds stability to the disc (helps the left fade), but L-to-R fights it. Stable + hyzer finds the left finish.
tips: Power down — downhill does a lot of work.
Hyzer + downhill stability produces reliable left fade.

short_right.disc_explanation: L-to-R pushes right, downhill extends flight. Understable on flat curves right and carries.
tips: Power way down — this carries very far downhill.
Mid disc gives more control for a short-right downhill shot.

long_left.disc_explanation: L-to-R fights a long-left shot. Downhill helps. Overstable + hyzer + downhill stability = left finish despite the wind.
tips: The downhill helps you on this shot.
Aim well right and let the hyzer and downhill bring it left.

long_right.disc_explanation: L-to-R pushes right, downhill extends. Understable disc carries far right — big sweeping right shot.
tips: Extreme distance and right carry.
Power way down.

s_curve.disc_explanation: L-to-R turns disc right first (S start). Downhill extends flight. Stable disc fades left at end for the return curve.
tips: Great S-curve conditions.
Power down — distance is not an issue downhill.

distance.disc_explanation: Downhill + L-to-R = lots of distance potential with right drift. Overstable prevents the drift from becoming a turnover.
tips: Best to aim left and let the wind drift it to center.
Power way down.

putting.disc_explanation: Downhill putt carries far, L-to-R drifts right. Overstable putter fights both for a reliable line.
tips: Aim left of the basket — wind drifts right.
Power way down — downhill + L-to-R carries far.

straight.disc_explanation: R-to-L wind pushes disc left, which aligns with the natural fade of a stable disc. Aim right and let the wind drift it back to center.
straight.aim_point: Slightly right of target — wind pushes disc back left to target
tips: Aim slightly right and let the wind drift it to center.
R-to-L is forgiving for RHBH players since it works with the natural fade.
Understable disc risks over-fading left.

short_left.disc_explanation: R-to-L pushes disc left. Stable disc on flat adds natural fade to wind push for a reliable left finish.
short_left.aim_point: Right of target — wind and fade both push left
tips: Easy shot in R-to-L wind — the wind works with your natural fade.
Don't over-aim right — the combined force can push past your target.

short_right.disc_explanation: R-to-L wind fights a right finish. Overstable disc's strong fade fights the wind push left, landing in a middle-right position.
short_right.aim_point: Far left of target — fight the wind
tips: Very difficult shot in R-to-L wind — going right against the wind.
Anhyzer + understable disc is extremely risky here — avoid.
Consider whether this shot is necessary or if there's a safer line.

long_left.disc_explanation: R-to-L wind amplifies left finish. Understable disc turns slightly right then fades left, and the wind exaggerates the left finish for a long-left flight.
long_left.aim_point: Right of target — combined wind and fade goes far left
tips: Be careful of the disc going too far left.
Stable disc might be the safer call in stronger R-to-L wind.

long_right.disc_explanation: R-to-L fights a right finish. Overstable on anhyzer starts right (anhyzer) and the wind pushes it back left — controlled flex shot.
tips: Difficult shot — the wind works against you the whole way.
Stable on anhyzer is very risky — disc won't recover and goes too far right.

s_curve.disc_explanation: Understable on anhyzer starts right (first curve of S), then R-to-L wind pushes it left while the understable fade also brings it left.
tips: Wind works against the S-curve — power up slightly to maintain disc speed.
Stable disc + anhyzer is a safer option for an S-curve in R-to-L.

distance.disc_explanation: R-to-L wind pushes disc left. Overstable driver fights the drift for maximum forward distance.
distance.aim_point: Right of landing zone — wind drifts left
tips: Aim right of your intended landing zone.
R-to-L doesn't reduce distance as much as a headwind.
Use overstable to prevent the disc from drifting too far left.

putting.disc_explanation: R-to-L wind drifts the putt left. A stable putter combined with aiming right compensates for the drift.
putting.aim_point: Right of center chains — let wind drift to center
tips: Aim right of the basket — wind drifts it left to center.
Overstable putter if the wind is strong.
Consistent follow-through fights the wind drift.

straight.disc_explanation: R-to-L pushes left, uphill promotes understability (right turn) — these partially cancel. Overstable driver in the middle keeps flight straight.
tips: The two forces partially cancel — overstable leans toward the wind.
Power up for uphill.
